title: express-fileupload with safeFileNames: false — uploaded filenames keep path/special characters (traversal)

params.passDetail: express-fileupload sanitizes uploaded filenames (safeFileNames is not false) — path separators and special characters are stripped before the name is used.
params.failDetail: express-fileupload is configured with safeFileNames: false, so an uploaded file's name is used as-is — including `../`, path separators, and null bytes. If that name is passed to mv()/a filesystem path, an attacker can traverse out of the upload directory and overwrite arbitrary files (e.g. a config or a served script). Set safeFileNames: true (ideally with a regex) and never build a path from an untrusted filename.
params.absentCallDetail: No fileUpload() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: fileUpload() does not set safeFileNames; note express-fileupload defaults it to false — set it to true explicitly.

provenance.note: safeFileNames defaults to false, so uploaded names keep `../` and separators. Code that builds a destination path from the untrusted name is a path-traversal write. It looks fine on normal uploads, so it ships.
